title: Magic Words

pages.text: Tom and Sam went to their friend Raju's birthday party.
After the party, all the friends played with Raju's toys.
Tom roughly asked Raju, "Hey, give me that toy jeep!"
Raju looked at him and said, "I can't give it to you."
Sam politely asked Raju, "Raju, can you please give me that blue jeep? I will play with it and return it to you."
Raju quickly gave him the blue jeep. Sam said, "Thanks, Raju!"
Tom complained to Sam, "He isn't giving me anything."
Sam said, "If you use the magic words politely, like 'please,' he'll give it to you."
Tom went back and asked politely, "Please, Raju, can you give me a toy jeep?"
Raju quickly gave him the jeep.
From that day on, Tom was never rude and always used the magic words: Please, Thank you, You're welcome, Excuse me, and I'm sorry.

moral: Kind and polite words make people happy and help build good friendships. Always remember to say 'please,' 'thank you,' 'you're welcome,' 'excuse me,' and 'I'm sorry.'
